_provenance: schema-derived from Option-A implementation contract (background-thread dispatch). No producer-computed values. Each entry describes a behavioral invariant asserted by tests/app/test_fleet_dismiss_background_dispatch.py.
description: Invariants for the background-thread dismiss contract: handler returns fast, background write lands, side-effect ban on request thread is preserved.

handler_latency_ms._note: Upper bound on acceptable synchronous handler duration. The background thread owns the I/O; the handler must return before the write completes.

background_dispatch._note: The handler must dispatch write_fleet_alert to a background thread, not call it directly.

durable_write._note: After the background thread completes, dismissed_at_et must be non-null. Assertions use a synchronization Event with a bounded timeout so tests are deterministic.

error_resilience._note: If write_fleet_alert raises (DB locked, full disk), the handler must still return 200. Failure is logged; the operator UI must not degrade on transient DB errors.

idempotency._note: Two rapid dismiss POSTs must both return 200. The effective write is idempotent (dismissed_at_et remains set; no crash on double-dismiss).

rapid_post_latency._note: Handler latency must remain stable across N rapid POSTs — no blocking accumulation from background threads.

sample_alert_row._note: A representative active alert row (not dismissed). Test setup uses this shape. Values are illustrative strings — not producer-computed.
